designSystem.styleGuidelines:
## Brand & Style
The design system for this automotive spa project transitions away from traditional car wash tropes toward a luxury editorial aesthetic. It targets a high-end clientele who view vehicle maintenance as an act of preservation and self-care. The brand personality is prestigious, meticulous, and calm.

The design style is **Editorial Minimalism** mixed with **High-Contrast Boldness**. It draws inspiration from luxury fashion journals and architectural digests, utilizing expansive whitespace to signify "breathing room" and premium service. The UI feels architectural and structured, mirroring the precision of detailing work. A signature "liquid" motif is introduced through the use of translucent layers and subtle gradients that mimic the surface of polished chrome or deep water.

## Layout & Spacing
This design system uses a **Fixed Grid** with an asymmetrical flair. The layout is based on a 12-column grid, but content should frequently "break" the grid or align to off-center axes to mimic high-end editorial design.

- **Whitespace:** Use aggressive vertical spacing (section gaps) to separate service tiers. 
- **Margins:** Large outer margins (64px+) on desktop create a "framed" look, similar to a page in a physical book.
- **Alignment:** Mix left-aligned text with centered headlines to create visual tension and interest. On mobile, transition to a single column with simplified 24px margins.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth in this design system is achieved through **Tonal Layering** and **Glassmorphism**, rather than traditional drop shadows.

- **Thin Silver Rules:** Use 1px borders in #E2E2E2 (at 20-40% opacity) to define sections. This replaces shadows and maintains the "sharp edge" philosophy.
- **Glassmorphism:** For overlays, navigation bars, or modals, use a backdrop blur (20px+) with a semi-transparent Deep Sea Teal or Obsidian fill. This evokes the "liquid" motif of high-end water treatment.
- **Zero Shadows:** Avoid soft blurs under cards. High-contrast color changes or hairlines are the only methods for indicating elevation.

## Components
Consistent styling for core UI elements:

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use a solid Deep Sea Teal (#004D40) fill with Silver text, sharp corners, and 18px horizontal padding. Secondary buttons are "Ghost" style with a 1px Silver border and uppercase Montserrat text.
- **Cards:** Cards for service packages (e.g., "Full Ceramic Coating") use a #1A1A1A background with a top 1px Silver hairline. There is no padding between the card edge and the edge of the image inside.
- **Lists:** Service menus use thin horizontal rules between items. The price should be aligned right in Playfair Display, while the service name is on the left in Montserrat.
- **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only. Labels float above the line in Montserrat Label-Caps style. The focus state turns the bottom border into Deep Sea Teal.
- **Chips:** For "Package Includes" tags, use small, square-edged boxes with 1px Silver outlines and 10px tracking on the text.
- **Signature Motif:** Use a background "Liquid Overlay"—a large, low-opacity SVG wave or a blurred teal orb that moves slowly in the background to provide a sense of luxury and flow.

---

## Brand & Style
The design system for this automotive spa project transitions away from traditional car wash tropes toward a luxury editorial aesthetic. It targets a high-end clientele who view vehicle maintenance as an act of preservation and self-care. The brand personality is prestigious, meticulous, and calm.

The design style is **Editorial Minimalism** mixed with **High-Contrast Boldness**. It draws inspiration from luxury fashion journals and architectural digests, utilizing expansive whitespace to signify "breathing room" and premium service. The UI feels architectural and structured, mirroring the precision of detailing work. A signature "liquid" motif is introduced through the use of translucent layers and subtle gradients that mimic the surface of polished chrome or deep water.

## Colors
The palette is rooted in the "Midnight & Chrome" concept, designed to evoke the atmosphere of a high-end garage at night.

- **Obsidian Black (#0A0A0A):** The foundational canvas. It provides a deep, infinite backdrop that allows photography and metallic accents to pop.
- **Metallic Silver (#E2E2E2):** Used for primary typography and thin "hairline" rules. It mimics the sheen of stainless steel and chrome.
- **Deep Sea Teal (#004D40):** A sophisticated accent color used sparingly for calls to action, active states, and to represent the advanced water filtration systems of the spa.
- **Surface Charcoal (#1A1A1A):** Used for secondary containers and subtle differentiation between sections without breaking the dark-mode immersion.

## Typography
The typography system relies on a sharp contrast between a romantic, high-contrast serif and a modern, geometric sans-serif.

- **Headlines:** Playfair Display is utilized for all major headings. Its delicate serifs and thick-and-thin strokes convey "Spa" luxury. Large display sizes should use tighter letter spacing to feel like a magazine cover.
- **Body & Labels:** Montserrat is the workhorse. To maintain the "Auto" feel, body text uses a lighter weight (300) with generous tracking (letter spacing) to feel airy and precise. 
- **Labels:** All small labels and navigation items should be set in Montserrat Uppercase with high tracking to emphasize the technical, professional nature of the service.

## Layout & Spacing
This design system uses a **Fixed Grid** with an asymmetrical flair. The layout is based on a 12-column grid, but content should frequently "break" the grid or align to off-center axes to mimic high-end editorial design.

- **Whitespace:** Use aggressive vertical spacing (section gaps) to separate service tiers. 
- **Margins:** Large outer margins (64px+) on desktop create a "framed" look, similar to a page in a physical book.
- **Alignment:** Mix left-aligned text with centered headlines to create visual tension and interest. On mobile, transition to a single column with simplified 24px margins.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth in this design system is achieved through **Tonal Layering** and **Glassmorphism**, rather than traditional drop shadows.

- **Thin Silver Rules:** Use 1px borders in #E2E2E2 (at 20-40% opacity) to define sections. This replaces shadows and maintains the "sharp edge" philosophy.
- **Glassmorphism:** For overlays, navigation bars, or modals, use a backdrop blur (20px+) with a semi-transparent Deep Sea Teal or Obsidian fill. This evokes the "liquid" motif of high-end water treatment.
- **Zero Shadows:** Avoid soft blurs under cards. High-contrast color changes or hairlines are the only methods for indicating elevation.

## Shapes
The shape language is defined by **Precision and Sharpness**.

- **Square Corners:** All buttons, containers, cards, and input fields must have a 0px border radius. This reflects the "Detail" in detailing—precise, controlled, and sharp.
- **Hairlines:** Vertical and horizontal silver lines should be used to separate content, creating a grid-like structure that feels like architectural blueprints.

## Components
Consistent styling for core UI elements:

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use a solid Deep Sea Teal (#004D40) fill with Silver text, sharp corners, and 18px horizontal padding. Secondary buttons are "Ghost" style with a 1px Silver border and uppercase Montserrat text.
- **Cards:** Cards for service packages (e.g., "Full Ceramic Coating") use a #1A1A1A background with a top 1px Silver hairline. There is no padding between the card edge and the edge of the image inside.
- **Lists:** Service menus use thin horizontal rules between items. The price should be aligned right in Playfair Display, while the service name is on the left in Montserrat.
- **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only. Labels float above the line in Montserrat Label-Caps style. The focus state turns the bottom border into Deep Sea Teal.
- **Chips:** For "Package Includes" tags, use small, square-edged boxes with 1px Silver outlines and 10px tracking on the text.
- **Signature Motif:** Use a background "Liquid Overlay"—a large, low-opacity SVG wave or a blurred teal orb that moves slowly in the background to provide a sense of luxury and flow.

screens.prompt: High-end automotive detailing close-up. A black luxury sedan being meticulously hand-washed with premium microfiber. Focus on water beads reflecting a deep obsidian paint finish with a "Deep Sea Teal" tint. Lighting is dramatic, cinematic, and moody, like a luxury watch or fashion editorial. Minimalist background. High contrast, sharp edges, metallic reflections. "Midnight & Chrome" aesthetic.
screens.title: High-end automotive detailing close-up. A black luxury sedan being meticulously hand-washed with premium microfiber. Focus on water beads reflecting a deep obsidian paint finish with a "Deep Sea Teal" tint. Lighting is dramatic, cinematic, and moody, like a luxury watch or fashion editorial. Minimalist background. High contrast, sharp edges, metallic reflections. "Midnight & Chrome" aesthetic.

screens.name: projects/1744506198664825685/screens/3835df32024d4d718e2e43b78cca36af
screens.prompt:
A bespoke, editorial homepage for 'Da Wash Auto Spa' in Mobile, Alabama. Theme: 'Midnight & Chrome' — deep obsidian backgrounds, silver rules, and 'Deep Sea Teal' highlights. High-contrast serif headings (Playfair Display) and wide-tracked sans-serif body. Structure & Layout: 1. Header: Minimalist TopNavBar with (251) 303-4959 in the top-right and a 'BOOK APPOINTMENT' primary button. 2. Hero: Asymmetrical composition. Large-scale high-contrast serif text: 'The Art of the Detail.' against a cinematic background image {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_5}}. Subtitle: 'Preserving automotive masterpieces in Mobile since 2024.' 3. #Credibility: A thin-ruled horizontal section with metrics: '500+ Masterpieces Restored', 'Ceramic Pro Certified', 'Bespoke Care'. Monochromatic styling. 4. Services: An editorial grid with {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_3}}. Packages: 'Signature Spa Wash', 'Ceramic Protection', 'Full Interior Restoration'. Each item uses a vertical typographic layout and thin silver borders. 5. About: A large text block with generous whitespace. Focus on the 'Precision over Speed' philosophy. Includes {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_4}}. 6. #Visit: A section titled 'The Atelier' with address (Mobile, United States) and the required empty map slot:
. 7. Contact: A minimalist form with silver underlines for inputs. Fields: Name, Vehicle, Preferred Service, Message. 8. Footer: Architectural layout with copyright info and links. Visual Motif: Subtle vertical lines (rules) and high-contrast photography. No rounded corners. Smooth scroll enabled. Full semantic HTML document.

spacing:
  unit: 8px
  container-max: 1440px
  gutter: 24px
  margin-desktop: 64px
  margin-mobile: 24px
  section-gap: 128px
---

## Brand & Style
The design system for this automotive spa project transitions away from traditional car wash tropes toward a luxury editorial aesthetic. It targets a high-end clientele who view vehicle maintenance as an act of preservation and self-care. The brand personality is prestigious, meticulous, and calm.

The design style is **Editorial Minimalism** mixed with **High-Contrast Boldness**. It draws inspiration from luxury fashion journals and architectural digests, utilizing expansive whitespace to signify "breathing room" and premium service. The UI feels architectural and structured, mirroring the precision of detailing work. A signature "liquid" motif is introduced through the use of translucent layers and subtle gradients that mimic the surface of polished chrome or deep water.

## Colors
The palette is rooted in the "Midnight & Chrome" concept, designed to evoke the atmosphere of a high-end garage at night.

- **Obsidian Black (#0A0A0A):** The foundational canvas. It provides a deep, infinite backdrop that allows photography and metallic accents to pop.
- **Metallic Silver (#E2E2E2):** Used for primary typography and thin "hairline" rules. It mimics the sheen of stainless steel and chrome.
- **Deep Sea Teal (#004D40):** A sophisticated accent color used sparingly for calls to action, active states, and to represent the advanced water filtration systems of the spa.
- **Surface Charcoal (#1A1A1A):** Used for secondary containers and subtle differentiation between sections without breaking the dark-mode immersion.

## Typography
The typography system relies on a sharp contrast between a romantic, high-contrast serif and a modern, geometric sans-serif.

- **Headlines:** Playfair Display is utilized for all major headings. Its delicate serifs and thick-and-thin strokes convey "Spa" luxury. Large display sizes should use tighter letter spacing to feel like a magazine cover.
- **Body & Labels:** Montserrat is the workhorse. To maintain the "Auto" feel, body text uses a lighter weight (300) with generous tracking (letter spacing) to feel airy and precise. 
- **Labels:** All small labels and navigation items should be set in Montserrat Uppercase with high tracking to emphasize the technical, professional nature of the service.

## Layout & Spacing
This design system uses a **Fixed Grid** with an asymmetrical flair. The layout is based on a 12-column grid, but content should frequently "break" the grid or align to off-center axes to mimic high-end editorial design.

- **Whitespace:** Use aggressive vertical spacing (section gaps) to separate service tiers. 
- **Margins:** Large outer margins (64px+) on desktop create a "framed" look, similar to a page in a physical book.
- **Alignment:** Mix left-aligned text with centered headlines to create visual tension and interest. On mobile, transition to a single column with simplified 24px margins.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth in this design system is achieved through **Tonal Layering** and **Glassmorphism**, rather than traditional drop shadows.

- **Thin Silver Rules:** Use 1px borders in #E2E2E2 (at 20-40% opacity) to define sections. This replaces shadows and maintains the "sharp edge" philosophy.
- **Glassmorphism:** For overlays, navigation bars, or modals, use a backdrop blur (20px+) with a semi-transparent Deep Sea Teal or Obsidian fill. This evokes the "liquid" motif of high-end water treatment.
- **Zero Shadows:** Avoid soft blurs under cards. High-contrast color changes or hairlines are the only methods for indicating elevation.

## Shapes
The shape language is defined by **Precision and Sharpness**.

- **Square Corners:** All buttons, containers, cards, and input fields must have a 0px border radius. This reflects the "Detail" in detailing—precise, controlled, and sharp.
- **Hairlines:** Vertical and horizontal silver lines should be used to separate content, creating a grid-like structure that feels like architectural blueprints.

## Components
Consistent styling for core UI elements:

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use a solid Deep Sea Teal (#004D40) fill with Silver text, sharp corners, and 18px horizontal padding. Secondary buttons are "Ghost" style with a 1px Silver border and uppercase Montserrat text.
- **Cards:** Cards for service packages (e.g., "Full Ceramic Coating") use a #1A1A1A background with a top 1px Silver hairline. There is no padding between the card edge and the edge of the image inside.
- **Lists:** Service menus use thin horizontal rules between items. The price should be aligned right in Playfair Display, while the service name is on the left in Montserrat.
- **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only. Labels float above the line in Montserrat Label-Caps style. The focus state turns the bottom border into Deep Sea Teal.
- **Chips:** For "Package Includes" tags, use small, square-edged boxes with 1px Silver outlines and 10px tracking on the text.
- **Signature Motif:** Use a background "Liquid Overlay"—a large, low-opacity SVG wave or a blurred teal orb that moves slowly in the background to provide a sense of luxury and flow.

screens.title: Da Wash Auto Spa - Bespoke Homepage

text:
I have completed the bespoke homepage for **Da Wash Auto Spa**. 

This design intentionally moves away from standard automotive templates, adopting a high-end "Midnight & Chrome" editorial aesthetic. I’ve focused on the concept of "The Art of the Detail," treating vehicle maintenance as a restoration process rather than a simple utility.

### Key Design Decisions:
- **Visual Identity:** A palette of deep obsidian and metallic silver, accented by "Deep Sea Teal" to represent precision and high-end water treatment.
- **Editorial Layout:** I used asymmetrical grids, generous whitespace, and high-contrast serif typography (Playfair Display) to evoke the feel of a luxury fashion or architecture magazine.
- **Bespoke Motifs:** Thin silver rules (borders) and sharp, precise edges are used throughout to reinforce the business’s focus on meticulous detail and craftsmanship.
- **Custom Assets:** I generated cinematic, moody imagery focusing on texture and precision—water beading on obsidian paint, carbon fiber detailing, and macro product shots—to create a premium first impression.
- **Functional Sections:** The site includes a credibility bar with key metrics, a structured service atelier, an "About" section focused on philosophy over speed, and a minimalist contact inquiry form.

The code is fully production-ready, featuring semantic HTML, responsive CSS with custom properties, and smooth scroll interactions.

How does this visual direction feel for the brand? We can refine the service descriptions or explore a more "mechanical-industrial" chrome look if you prefer.

suggestion: Make the 'Deep Sea Teal' accents more prominent
Switch to a more industrial 'Chrome & Carbon' aesthetic
Add a gallery section for past projects
